content.body: Noam Rosenthal I should have done it the other way around (so you could try it out), but you and Dominic Farolino now have access to the main branch on Fetch as well. I thought that was already the case, but apparently we have quite a few restrictions in place to prevent mishaps.

jjaschke: I noticed that disabled was not recalculated during post-insertion which seems like a bug
content.formatted_body: jjaschke: I noticed that disabled was not recalculated during post-insertion which seems like a bug

content.body: jjaschke: and I think I also kinda agree with one of your earlier comments about just using microtask for all the things; seems that would make it more predictable

content.body:
https://ladybird.org/posts/changing-how-we-develop-ladybird/

> We will no longer accept public pull requests. From now on, code changes to the Ladybird codebase will only be introduced by project maintainers.
content.formatted_body:
https://ladybird.org/posts/changing-how-we-develop-ladybird/

We will no longer accept public pull requests. From now on, code changes to the Ladybird codebase will only be introduced by project maintainers.

content.body: annevk: And also the implementations would likely become simpler if everything's a microtask
content.formatted_body: annevk: And also the implementations would likely become simpler if everything's a microtask

content.formatted_body: jjaschke: yeah I pushed a commit to jarhar's PR to show that; we wouldn't need post-connection steps

content.body: emilio: I think I'll add a test later today for the select clip issue; looks like we had some leftover imperative styling in WebKit that should be restricted to the native appearance. Looks like an easy fix
